title: Martinitoren
description: The Martinitoren, Groningen's iconic 97-metre church tower, rises above the city skyline. It has been the symbol of Groningen since the 15th century.

title: City centre street life
description: Cyclists and pedestrians on a Groningen street with the Der Aa-kerk tower catching the golden afternoon light. Groningen is one of the most bike-friendly cities in the world.

title: Reitdiephaven
description: The colorful wooden houses at Reitdiephaven, a modern marina district on Groningen's northern edge. The vibrant architecture has become one of the city's most photographed spots.

title: Sunset from Forum Groningen
description: The Martinitoren silhouette against a vivid sunset sky, seen from the rooftop terrace of Forum Groningen, the city's striking cultural center opened in 2019.

title: Hoge der Aa canal
description: Historic tall ships moored along the Hoge der Aa canal with the Der Aa-kerk tower in the background. This canal has been the heart of Groningen's trade since the Middle Ages.

title: Cycling culture
description: Bikes parked along Broerstraat, one of Groningen's main streets, with the historic Harmoniegebouw in the background. Over 60% of all trips in Groningen's centre are made by bicycle.

title: Martinitoren in spring
description: The clock face and copper crown of the Martinitoren framed by fresh spring leaves. The tower stands 97 metres tall and offers panoramic views across the city and surrounding countryside.

title: Academie Minerva at night
description: A cyclist passes the illuminated facade of Academie Minerva, Groningen's art academy, housed in a striking 19th-century building on Praediniussingel.

title: Summer on the canals
description: A tree-lined canal in Groningen's historic centre on a summer day, with houseboats and the Der Aa-kerk tower visible in the distance.

title: Winter light on the Hoge der Aa
description: Historic ships and canal houses along the Hoge der Aa in winter, with the Der Aa-kerk reflected in the still water. The old harbor district is one of Groningen's most picturesque areas.

title: Martinikerk under dramatic skies
description: An aerial view of the Martinikerk (St. Martin's Church) and Martinitoren under brooding clouds. The church dates back to the 13th century and dominates the Grote Markt square.

title: Autumn on Verlengde Visserstraat
description: Canal view near Verlengde Visserstraat in early autumn, with boats moored along tree-lined quays and the A-kerk tower in the background.
